title: blechnum squamatum
intro: Blechnum squamatum, also known as the water fern or rock fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Blechnaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Asia, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Blechnum squamatum include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, although it is essential to note that its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
